title: Fakoli Style Principles Ledger
description: Governed ledger of the Fakoli Style operating-model principles. Each entry declares a principle, the failure it prevents, and an honest lifecycle status (proven, asserted, aspirational) with status-appropriate evidence.

version.description: Semantic version of the ledger document shape.

principles.description: The principle entries. Sortable by credibility_risk then status without ambiguity (every entry carries both).

embodiment.description: A concrete place where the principle is mechanized in the codebase.

plugin.description: The plugin that embodies the principle.

ref.description: An existing repo-relative file path that mechanizes the principle.

mechanism.description: One line naming the mechanism at that ref.

why.description: One sentence naming the specific failure the rule prevents.

status.description: Lifecycle status. proven: machine-verified. asserted: claimed with a pointer but not yet machine-verified. aspirational: not yet built.

credibility_risk.description: How load-bearing the claim is to the project's credibility if it turns out false.

proof.description: Pointer to evidence. For proven/asserted, a repo file path optionally suffixed with ::Class::method. Absent on aspirational entries.

embodied_in.description: Where the principle is mechanized. Required and non-empty for proven/asserted.

open_work.description: The work that would raise the status. Required for aspirational.

allOf.description: proven and asserted entries must carry a proof pointer and a non-empty embodied_in array.
aspirational entries must declare open_work and must not assert a proof pointer or embodiment.
